subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Run the Measurement Workshop by comparing length, height, weight, and repeated units.
lesson.concept_explanation: Measurement Foundations means using a math model, saying what you see, and checking that the answer fits.
lesson.common_misconception: Length tells how long something is from end to end.
lesson.real_world_connection: Run the Measurement Workshop by comparing length, height, weight, and repeated units.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model and careful counting to solve measurement foundations problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use measurement comparison to reason carefully before choosing or writing an answer.

worked_examples.text: Watch the model first, then match it to the answer for measurement foundations.
Demo: count, compare, or match each part before answering.

hint_ladder: Say the math words out loud.
Check your answer by doing the action again.

guided_practice.prompt: Which is longer, A or B?
hints: Look at the model carefully.
Count or compare one step at a time.
Check that your answer matches the question.
guided_practice.explanation: The correct answer is A because the model matches the prompt.
guided_practice.feedback: Nice work: A matches the model.

misconception_bank.length_confusion: Length tells how long something is from end to end.
misconception_bank.height_confusion: Height tells how tall something is up and down.
misconception_bank.weight_size_confusion: Bigger does not always mean heavier.
misconception_bank.unit_iteration_error: Units must touch with no gaps or overlaps.
misconception_bank.comparison_error: Compare the same attribute on both objects.

questions.prompt: A pencil is covered by 4 same-size cubes with no gaps. How many cubes long is it?

Count or compare one step at a time.

story.text: Welcome to Measurement Workshop Quest. The workshop needs help comparing length, height, weight, and repeated units. Your goal is to measure and compare objects carefully. You will learn to line things up, use the same kind of unit, and explain which object is longer, taller, heavier, or the same. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about measurement foundations. Important words are length, height, weight, compare, unit, and measure. The measurement comparison model shows two objects, such as A and B, so you can compare them from end to end or top to bottom. A common mistake is comparing objects that are not lined up, so start at the same place before deciding. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt asks which is longer, A or B. Step one, read what attribute to compare: length. Step two, look at the model and line up the starting ends. Step three, see which object reaches farther from end to end. The final answer is A when A is the longer object. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how measurement compares two objects. Watch the model line up A and B, then compare only the attribute in the question. For example, if A stretches farther from end to end than B, A is longer. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the measurement strategy: line up the objects, compare the correct attribute, and use words like longer, taller, heavier, or same. The model shows two objects labeled A and B. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It is harder because the question may compare height, length, or repeated units, and the objects may look close. Use the same starting point and compare only what the question asks. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can compare measurements and explain your choice. Use the model, line up the objects, and decide which is longer, taller, heavier, or the same. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work in Measurement Workshop Quest. You practiced comparing length, height, weight, and units with careful measurement words. Those strategies help you measure fairly. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your measurement practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep building comparison skills. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
